project.objective: When and where did Anders Monsen marry Unna Halsteinsdatter, and what does the marriage record show?

questions.question: When and where did Anders Monsen (FamilySearch person LKFW-9XH) marry Unna Halsteinsdatter, and what information does the marriage record contain?
questions.rationale: The project objective is a single marriage-event question. Norwegian church books (kirkebøker) recording marriages from the 18th–19th centuries are well-represented on FamilySearch, making direct record search the logical first step. This question targets the marriage event directly and is testable: a marriage record (or its absence in available sources) will confirm or deny the fact.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: The primary indexed collection (FamilySearch Norway, Marriages, 1660-1926) was searched with two query variants including isPrincipal filtering. A direct marriage record was found and confirmed: Hamre prestegjeld church book entry, 25 Jun 1786, groom Anders Monsen, bride indexed as 'Urna Halsteinsdr' (Unna Halsteinsdatter). The digital image of the original church book page is accessible via FamilySearch (ark:/61903/1:2:MY3L-FH3). For 1786 Norwegian marriages, the church book is the sole contemporaneous record type — no civil registration existed until 1875 — so the single-source limitation reflects the historical record landscape, not an unsearched gap. Fallback items (pli_002, pli_003) were skipped because pli_001 succeeded; contextual/corroboration items (pli_004-007) were skipped because the question is directly and fully answered.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes. q_001 asked when, where, and what the marriage record shows. The Hamre prestegjeld church book entry (25 Jun 1786, Hamre, Hordaland, Norway) directly answers all three parts: date, place, and record content (groom Anders Monsen, bride Urna/Unna Halsteinsdr/Halsteinsdatter).
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch Norway, Marriages, 1660-1926 (collection 1468080) — the principal indexed source for 18th-century Norwegian church marriages — was searched with two variants. The record was found and is already FamilySearch-tree-attached to LKFW-9XH. Digital image of the original Hamre prestegjeld church book page is accessible.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: The FamilySearch database is a derivative index, but the accompanying digital image (ark:/61903/1:2:MY3L-FH3) provides direct access to the original Hamre prestegjeld kirkebøker. The derivative was confirmed against the underlying original image.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Single source (Hamre prestegjeld church book). For 1786 Norway, civil marriage registration did not exist; the parish register is the sole contemporaneous record type for marriages. No second independent source was found, but none could be expected to exist for this event type, time, and place. This limitation is noted and does not represent an unsearched gap.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Original church book entry (Hamre prestegjeld kirkebøker 1677-1928). The officiating minister or parish clerk recorded the marriage event on official duty — primary information for the date, place, and parties' names.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: No conflicting evidence found. The marriage date 25 Jun 1786 in the church book is consistent with Anders Monsen's existing tree fact (F1) and with Unna Halsteinsdatter's existing Couple relationship to LKFW-9XH (rel-anders-unna).
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low. The record is already attached to the FamilySearch tree person LKFW-9XH with a 5-star match. For 1786 Norwegian marriages, no alternative documentary source (civil record, newspaper, or state register) existed that could plausibly name a different marriage date or place.

items.rationale: Norway, Marriages, 1660–1926 (collection 1468080) is an indexed database of Norwegian church-book marriage entries. Both Anders and Unna were christened at Hamre kirke, making Hamre prestegjeld the highest-probability location for their marriage. An indexed search for Anders Monsen / Unna Halsteinsdatter here is the fastest path to a direct hit before resorting to browse-only image films.

items.jurisdiction: Hamre prestegjeld (Hamre og Meland parishes), Hordaland, Norway
items.rationale: FamilySearch Catalog item 47267 — Hamre prestegjeld kirkebøker 1677–1928 — contains the original parish registers (images) for Hamre and Meland. Until 1885, Meland was within Hamre clerical district. Browsing the marriage (viede) sections of the relevant volumes for 1770–1800 will catch any entry missed by the index due to spelling variation or transcription gap.

items.jurisdiction: Hamre and Meland parishes, Hordaland, Norway
items.rationale: Digitalarkivet hosts the same Hamre/Alversund/Meland church records (1750–1857) independently of FamilySearch. Using this as a parallel repository confirms whether a marriage entry exists when FamilySearch catalog images are unclear or partially digitized. Digitalarkivet URL: https://media.digitalarkivet.no/kb/browse?archives[]=no-a1450-07000000000996

FamilySearch Catalog item 58465 — Manger prestegjeld kirkebøker 1759–1923. Anders died and was buried at Manger/Radøy in 1821, suggesting the family may have relocated to that area before or soon after marriage. Checking Manger marriage registers for the same period covers the possibility that the wedding took place after the family moved to Åsebø/Manger rather than in Hamre.

Norway, Baptisms, 1634–1927 (collection 1467014) — indexed. Searching for children of Anders Monsen will surface baptism records that name both parents, establishing which parish the family used and triangulating the probable marriage date. Any child born after the expected marriage window also constrains the latest possible marriage date.

Norway Census, 1801 (collection 3733603) — indexed. The 1801 census lists all household members by name with age and occupation. Locating Anders Monsen and Unna Halsteinsdatter together confirms they were married, establishes where they were living c.1801, and shows family composition (children's ages help back-calculate the marriage year). This is a critical corroborating source and will anchor the family in a specific parish.

Nordhordaland deanery clerical probate records 1773–1810 (FamilySearch catalog 438920) cover Hamre, Manger, and associated parishes. Anders's father Mons Monsen died in 1779; his probate would name heirs including Anders and could confirm the family's location and the father's farm at the time — contextual information that corroborates which parish Anders was residing in when he married Unna. FAN-cluster item per BCG Standard 14.

log.notes: 125 results returned but all are records where Anders Monsen appears as a secondary person (parent/witness); no record where he is principal groom. Top match score only 0.028 — no confident match. Retrying with isPrincipal: true to filter to records where he is the groom.
Zero results with isPrincipal:true and spouseGivenName Unna. Possible causes: spouse name variant (Una/Unne/Unda), surname variant, or date range mismatch. Retrying without spouse name filter.

Rank-2 result (matchScore 0.632) already attached to subject LKFW-9XH: record ark:/61903/1:1:NW44-PM2. Record shows groom Anders Monsen, bride indexed as 'Urna Halsteinsdr' (variant of Unna Halsteinsdatter), married 25 Jun 1786 at Hamre, Hordaland, Norway. Spouse name variant explains earlier nil with spouseGivenName Unna. Image: https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:MY3L-FH3

sources.citation: "Norway, Marriages, 1660-1926," database, FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:MY3L-FH3 : 9 July 2026), entry for Anders Monsen, married 25 June 1786, Hamre, Hordaland, Norway; citing Hamre prestegjeld parish register.

citation_detail.what: Marriage register entry, Hamre prestegjeld kirkebøker (church books)

sources.notes: Index and digital image derived from Hamre prestegjeld original kirkebøker 1677-1928. Bride indexed as 'Urna Halsteinsdr' — variant/abbreviation of Unna Halsteinsdatter. Record already attached to FS tree person LKFW-9XH (5-star match, rank 2 by score 0.632).

assertions.informant: Urna Halsteinsdr (bride) — indexed variant of Unna Halsteinsdatter
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Bride's name appears in the FamilySearch index as 'Urna Halsteinsdr' — a transcription/abbreviation variant of 'Unna Halsteinsdatter'. The variation is in the derivative index, not in the original informant's statement: the bride herself stated her name, which would have been recorded in the original Hamre prestegjeld church book. Her christening record (27 May 1745, Hamre) independently corroborates the name as 'Unna Halsteinsdatter'. No material bias on the bride's own name reporting.

assertions.informant: officiating minister or parish clerk, Hamre prestegjeld

person_evidence.rationale: Groom persona 'Anders Monsen' matches LKFW-9XH exactly: same full name, Male, married 25 Jun 1786 at Hamre, Hordaland — the same parish where he was christened in 1759. At marriage he was ~27, a plausible age. FamilySearch attaches this record to LKFW-9XH with a 5-star tree match (log_003). No conflicting identifiers.

Bride persona indexed as 'Urna Halsteinsdr': 'Urna' is a transcription variant of 'Unna' (common in 18th-century Norwegian indexes); 'Halsteinsdr' is the standard abbreviation of 'Halsteinsdatter', matching KZHH-VTX's surname exactly. Female, christened 1745 at Hamre, making her ~41 at the 1786 marriage — unusual but historically attested. KZHH-VTX is already recorded as the Couple partner of LKFW-9XH (rel-anders-unna). The only discrepancy is the minor transcription variant on the given name, which does not override the surname, location, and spousal relationship agreement.

Marriage event assertion (groom role, 25 Jun 1786, Hamre, Hordaland) links to LKFW-9XH as the groom. This event fact (F1) is already recorded in LKFW-9XH's tree facts. The Hamre prestegjeld church book is the authoritative marriage register for this parish. Consistent with all other known data for Anders Monsen.

Marriage event assertion (relationship assertion — both parties). Per cardinality rule, a marriage event links to both the groom and the bride. KZHH-VTX (Unna Halsteinsdatter) is the bride in record ark:/61903/1:1:NW44-PM2, confirmed by the a_002 link. The Couple relationship rel-anders-unna already joins LKFW-9XH and KZHH-VTX in the tree; this assertion corroborates it with a dated, placed church book entry.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Searched FamilySearch 'Norway, Marriages, 1660–1926' (collection 1468080) with three progressive queries: (1) surname Monsen + given Anders + spouseGivenName Unna, 1775–1800 — 125 results, all secondary-role appearances (Anders as parent/witness), no principal groom match; (2) same with isPrincipal:true — 0 results (bride name 'Unna' not in index); (3) isPrincipal:true, no spouse filter, 1770–1800 — positive hit at rank 2 (match score 0.632), record ark:/61903/1:1:NW44-PM2, already FamilySearch-attached to LKFW-9XH. Fallback collections (Hamre prestegjeld browse-only images via FamilySearch catalog; Digitalarkivet) were skipped because the indexed collection returned a confirmed positive. Corroboration items (Manger prestegjeld church records pli_004, Norway Baptisms pli_005, Norway Census 1801 pli_006, Nordhordaland probate pli_007) were skipped because the question is directly answered by the marriage record. For 18th-century Norwegian marriages, the church book is the sole contemporaneous record type; civil registration was not established until 1875. The single-source limitation is structural, not a research gap.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Marriage of Anders Monsen and Unna Halsteinsdatter, 25 June 1786, Hamre, Hordaland, Norway

**Proof Summary — Probable**

**Conclusion.** The preponderance of evidence strongly indicates that Anders Monsen (born 1759, Håtuft, Meland, Hordaland, Norway; FamilySearch person LKFW-9XH) married Unna Halsteinsdatter (born May 1745, Hestdal, Meland, Hordaland, Norway; FamilySearch person KZHH-VTX) on 25 June 1786 at Hamre, Hordaland, Norway, as recorded in the Hamre prestegjeld parish register.

**The Marriage Record.** One original source directly addresses this question: an entry in "Norway, Marriages, 1660–1926," database and digital images, FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:MY3L-FH3 : 9 July 2026), for Anders Monsen, married 25 June 1786, Hamre, Hordaland, Norway; citing Hamre prestegjeld parish register, Statsarkivet, Bergen.¹ The indexed entry (record ARK: ark:/61903/1:1:NW44-PM2) is a **derivative source** — a digitized index derived from the Hamre prestegjeld kirkebøker (church books, 1677–1928); the digital image of the underlying original church book page is accessible at the image ARK above.

The informant for the marriage event (date, place, and parties' names) is the officiating minister or parish clerk of Hamre prestegjeld, recording on official duty — **primary information** for the event. The assertions drawn from this record are **direct evidence** that answer the research question.

The groom is indexed as **Anders Monsen**. The bride is indexed as **Urna Halsteinsdr** — a transcription variant of Unna Halsteinsdatter. The given name "Urna" for "Unna" reflects a well-documented 18th-century Norwegian handwriting ambiguity; "Halsteinsdr" is the standard abbreviated form of the patronymic "Halsteinsdatter." This variant does not constitute a conflict; it is the expected derivative-index transcription artifact. The bride's christening record (27 May 1745, Hamre) independently corroborates the name as "Unna Halsteinsdatter."²

**Identity Correlation.** The groom persona matches LKFW-9XH on every available identifier: exact name, male sex, age approximately 27 at marriage (born 1759 — plausible), and the Hamre prestegjeld parish where he was christened on 7 April 1759.³ FamilySearch's own matching algorithm independently attaches this record to LKFW-9XH with a five-star tree confidence. The bride persona matches KZHH-VTX on name (variant explained above), female sex, age approximately 41 at marriage (born May 1745 — historically attested), christening location (Hamre area, 27 May 1745), and the existing Couple relationship linking her to LKFW-9XH in the FamilySearch tree.

**Research Scope.** The principal indexed collection for 18th-century Norwegian church marriages — "Norway, Marriages, 1660–1926" (FamilySearch collection 1468080) — was searched with three progressively refined queries. The positive match was confirmed at the third query (isPrincipal:true, no spouse-name filter, 1770–1800). Browse-only image fallbacks (Hamre prestegjeld kirkebøker via FamilySearch catalog; Digitalarkivet) were not searched because the indexed collection returned a confirmed positive hit. For Norwegian marriages prior to civil registration (established 1875), the parish church book is the sole contemporaneous record type; no second independent source type existed for this event.

**Confidence Tier: Probable.** The Genealogical Proof Standard requires two or more independent original sources with primary information for a conclusion at the "proved" tier. Research for this question yielded one authoritative original source — the Hamre prestegjeld church book entry — and exhaustive research was declared because no second contemporaneous source type existed for 1786 Norwegian marriages. The evidence is direct, uncontradicted, and sourced from the authoritative original register; the probable tier reflects this structural single-source limitation, not any research gap, conflicting evidence, or unresolved discrepancy.

---
¹ "Norway, Marriages, 1660-1926," database, FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:MY3L-FH3 : 9 July 2026), entry for Anders Monsen, married 25 June 1786, Hamre, Hordaland, Norway; citing Hamre prestegjeld parish register.
² Hamre prestegjeld, christening 27 May 1745, Unna Halsteinsdatter; cited in tree person KZHH-VTX.
³ Den norske kirke, Hamre parish, "Ministerialbok, 1750–1761," Døbte, page 103, entry 24, Anders, 7 April 1759; digital images, Arkivverket.no (Digitalarkivet.no); cited in tree person LKFW-9XH.
